# Vimtico

> Native macOS PostgreSQL client with Vim keybindings. A fast, keyboard-driven alternative to Postico.

Vimtico is a free, open-source macOS application for working with PostgreSQL databases. It combines a native SwiftUI interface with full Vim mode support in the query editor, making it ideal for developers who prefer keyboard-driven workflows.

## Key facts

- Native macOS app built with SwiftUI
- Full Vim emulation: normal, insert, visual, visual-line, and command modes
- 40+ Vim motions, operator+motion combos, text objects, dot repeat, yank register
- Cell-based Vim navigation in query results (j/k/h/l, y to copy)
- Nord theme (dark and light variants) with custom theme support
- SQL syntax highlighting and validation via EXPLAIN
- Autocomplete: rule-based (offline) or API-based (OpenAI, Anthropic Claude)
- Connection management with auto-reconnect
- Schema browser with table info (columns, types, nullable, defaults, PKs, row count, size)
- Resizable editor/results pane with draggable divider
- Filter sidebar and results with / (Vim-style search)
- Configurable font size, zoom (Cmd+/-/0) across all panes
- Installed via Homebrew: `brew install --cask mathieux51/tap/vimtico`
- MIT licensed

## Comparisons

- Like Postico but with Vim keybindings and free
- Like pgAdmin but native macOS, fast, keyboard-driven
- Like DBeaver but lightweight, Mac-native, Vim-focused

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/mathieux51/vimtico
- Releases: https://github.com/mathieux51/vimtico/releases
- Homebrew tap: https://github.com/mathieux51/homebrew-tap
- Install: `brew install --cask mathieux51/tap/vimtico`
